title: alnus trabeculosa
intro: Alnus trabeculosa, also known as the White Alder or Grey Alder, is a species of deciduous tree native to the mountainous regions of North America. The bark and leaves of the Alnus trabeculosa plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat various health conditions such as respiratory issues and wounds. The medicinal properties of Alnus trabeculosa have been found to have health benefits including reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and acting as a natural antiseptic. Alnus trabeculosa can be prepared in herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being used in salves and ointments for topical application.
